13.5 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A Typical PC Bus Structure

# Page 6^page=6
13.6 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A Typical PC Bus Structure PCI(Peripheral Component Interconnect)是英特尔推出的局部总线。 地址与数据分时复用，支持即插即用 、中断共享等功能。PCI Express是新一 代的总线接口，称之为第三代I/O总线技术。接口根据总线位宽不同而有所差异 ，包括X1、X4、X8以及X16，能够提供8GB/s的带宽，

# Page 7^page=7
13.7 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Controller

# Page 8^page=8
13.8 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Controllers u Controller's tasks Ø Control the physical operation of the device Ø convert serial bit stream to block of bytes Ø perform error correction as necessary Ø Exchange data with CPU via registers u可编址设备，连接多个设备时，每一个地址对应一个设备 Device I/O Port Locations on PCs

# Page 9^page=9
13.9 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Controllers u By writing into the registers: OS can command it to deliver or accept data, Or switch the device on or off u By reading from the registers: OS can learn the status of the device u In addition, controllers often have buffers Ø Which the OS can read or write, Eg: a video controller may have Video RAM,A data buffer programs can write or read

# Page 10^page=10
13.10 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Software I/O Approaches uProgrammed I/O(Polling) uInterrupt-driven I/O uDMA I/O uChannel I/O

# Page 11^page=11
13.11 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Programmed I/O uCPU wait for the I/O to complete uThis is called Polling or Busy waiting Ø simple to program

# Page 12^page=12
13.12 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Programmed I/O(Polling) u The host repeatedly reads the busy bit until the bit becomes clear; u The host issues an I/O instruction to controller; u The controller sets the busy bit; u The controller does the I/O operation by controlling the device; u Once the I/O operation is done successfully, the controller clears the busy bit to inform the host; u Busy-wait cycle to wait for I/O from device; 读 外 设 端 口 准 备 好 ? 数 据 传 输 Y N 实现查询方式输入数据的程序如下: TEST: IN AL, STATUS;读入状态 AND AL, 01H;测试是否准备好 JE TEST;未好，继续测试 IN AL, n;从端口n读入数据 MOV BUFFER, AL;存数据到缓冲区 How about output?

# Page 13^page=13
13.13 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Programmed I/O Steps in printing a string

# Page 14^page=14
13.14 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Interrupts u Interrupt----the hardware mechanism that enables a device to notify the CPU u The basic interrupt mechanism works as follows: Ø The device controller raises an interrupt by asserting a signal on the interrupt request line Ø CPU catches the interrupt and dispatches it to the interrupt handler Ø Interrupt handler performs necessary processing and executes a return from interrupt instruction to return CPU u Interrupt vector to dispatch interrupt to correct handler Ø Based on priority Ø Some nonmaskable u Interrupt mechanism also used for exceptions

# Page 15^page=15
13.15 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Interrupt-Driven I/O CPU 磁盘 3. CPU回应 中断 1. 设备发出中断 2. 控制器 发出中断 中断 控制器 总线 u CPU setups and start I/O operation u CPU goes off to do other things u When I/O is done, CPU is interrupted u CPU handles the interrupt u CPU resumes interrupted operation Interrupt-Driven I/O Cycle

# Page 16^page=16
13.16 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 用 户 程 序 请 求 I / O 操 作 系 统 保 护 现 场 启 动 I / O指 令 I / O设 备 运 行 本 次 操 作 完 成 继 续 P, 或 转 Q 发 中 断 响 应 并 处 理 中 断 保 护 Q的 现 场 进 行 下 一 次 I / O 程 序 P … … 程 序 P/ Q . . .

13.18 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 8048 8255A 键盘 接 口 电 路 CPU执行程序 键盘中断 按一 个键 键盘中断处理程序 09H中断 保存现场 通过8255读扫描码 通过8255使接口电路复位 识别扫描码并转换成扩展 ASCI I 码送入键盘BUF 恢复现场 I RET 响应中断 . . . 继续 U70 注：U70为中断请求触发器 加电自检，产生1位 状态码和8位扫描码

# Page 19^page=19
13.19 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition I/O Using DMA(Direct Memory Access) u An obvious con for interrupt-driven I/O is the frequent interrupts u The solution is to use DMA, Idea is to let DMA controller handle the I/O one character at a time, In other words, busy wait with DMA. u Bypasses CPU to transfer data directly between I/O device and memory CPU 地址 计数 控制 主存 磁盘 缓冲 区 1. CPU对 DMA 控制器编程 2. DMA发出数 据传输请求 3. 数据传输 4. 回应DMA 5. DMA发 出中断 磁盘 控制器 DMA 控制器 总线 Operation of a DMA transfer CPU向控制器发出启动DMA通知和有关参数 控制器向内存发出访问请求 访问内存( 读\ 写) 计数器减1 结束否 发中断 Y N

# Page 20^page=20
13.20 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Six Step Process to Perform DMA Transfer

13.21 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 用户程序 请求I / O 操作系统 保护现场 初始化DMA控制器, 启动I / O指令 I / O设备 该字节由DMA送到指 定内存缓冲区 挂起P, 调度Q运行 发中断 响应并处理中断 保护Q的现场 磁盘控制器向DMA控制器 发送一个字节数据 内存地址+1; 字节计数- 1 DC=0? Y N 继续 内存缓冲首地址送MAR 传送数据字节数送DC 读或写命令送CR 程序P . . . . . . 程序Q . . .

# Page 22^page=22
13.22 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Issues with DMA uHow to access the bus? Ø Cycle stealing(利用CPU不访存的周期实现DMA操作，比如 CPU在执行乘法指令时。当I/O设备发出DMA请求时，便挪 用或窃取总线占用权一个或几个主存周期进行数据传输) uWhere to store the data? Ø Directly into memory or in DMA buffer? uHow to address the memory? Ø Virtual or physical address?

# Page 23^page=23
13.23 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Channel I/O 字节交叉方式传送数据 连接多个设备，一次只 能一个设备进行工作 多个设备同时工作，但只允 许一个设备进行传输型操作 u A special processor, also called I/O processor; u Executes channel program and completes a set of I/O operations at a time u Transfers data between devices and memory

# Page 24^page=24
13.24 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 用户程序 请求I / O 操作系统 保护现场 启动I / O指令 设备控制 器和设备 挂起P, 调度Q运行 发中断 响应并处理中断 保护Q的现场 通道程序结 束? Y N 继续 通道 组织通道程序, 保存 通道程序始址 执行通道程 序, 控制I / O 设备操作, 完成数据在 设备和内存 间的传递 执行通 道指令 规定的 操作 程序P . . . . . . 程序Q . . .

Y N 继 续 内 存 缓 冲 首 地 址 送 MAR 传 送 数 据 字 节 数 送 DC 读 或 写 命 令 送 CR 程 序 P . . . . . . 程 序 Q . . . 用 户 程 序 请 求 I / O 操 作 系 统 保 护 现 场 启 动 I / O指 令 设 备 控 制 器 和 设 备 挂 起 P, 调 度 Q运 行 发 中 断 响 应 并 处 理 中 断 保 护 Q的 现 场 通 道 程 序 结 束 ? # Page 26^page=26
13.26 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Application I/O Interface u OS can abstract away the detailed differences in I/O devices by identifying a few general kinds. u Each general kind is accessed through a standardized set of functions – an interface. u Device-driver layer hides differences among I/O controllers from kernel

# Page 27^page=27
13.27 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A Kernel I/O Structure

# Page 28^page=28
13.28 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition I/O device types u I/O设备类型繁多，从OS观点看，其性能指标有： p 数据传输速率；数据的传输单位；设备共享属性等 u 按传输速率分类 ： p 低速设备：每秒几个至数百个字节。例如：键盘、 鼠标器等设备。 p 中速设备：每秒数千个至数万个字节。典型设备有行式打印机、激光打 印机等。 p 高速设备：传输速率在数百K个字节至数十兆字节。 磁盘机、光盘机等。 u 按信息交换的单位分类 p 块设备；字符设备； u 按设备的共享属性分类 p 独占设备； p 共享设备； p 虚拟设备：在一类设备上模拟另一类设备，常用共享设备模拟独占设备， 用高速设备模拟低速设备，被模拟的设备称为虚拟设备

# Page 29^page=29
13.29 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Characteristics of I/O Devices

# Page 30^page=30
13.30 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Block and Character Devices u Block devices include disk drives Ø Commands include read, write, seek Ø Raw I/O or file-system access Ø Memory-mapped file access possible u Character devices include keyboards, mice, serial ports Ø Commands include get, put Ø Libraries layered on top allow line editing(通过库行编辑)

# Page 31^page=31
13.31 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Network Devices u Varying enough from block and character to have own interface u Unix and Windows NT/9x/2000 include socket interface u Also, many other approaches to inter-process communication and network communication Ø pipes, FIFOs, streams, message queues, mailboxes

# Page 32^page=32
13.32 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Clocks and Timers u Hardware clocks and timers provide 3 basic functions: Ø Give the current time Ø Give the elapsed time Ø Set a timer to trigger operation X at time T u The system calls implementing these functions are not standardized across operating systems.

# Page 33^page=33
13.33 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Clocks and Timers u Programmable interval timer used for timings, periodic interrupts. The hardware to measure elapsed time and trigger operations： Ø The scheduler uses it to generate an interrupt that will preempt a process at the end of its time slice Ø The disk subsystem uses it to invoke the flushing of dirty cache buffers to disk periodically Ø The network subsystem uses it to cancel operations that are proceeding too slowly because of network congestions or failures u The OS may also provide an interface for user process to use timer.

# Page 34^page=34
13.34 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Blocking and Nonblocking I/O(sys call) u Blocking - process suspended until I/O completed Ø Easy to use and understand Ø Insufficient for some needs u Nonblocking - I/O call returns as much as available Ø User interface, data copy (buffered I/O) Ø Implemented via multi-threading Ø Returns quickly with count of bytes read or written u Asynchronous - process runs while I/O executes(process) Ø Difficult to use Ø I/O subsystem signals process when I/O completed

# Page 35^page=35
13.35 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Two I/O Methods Synchronous Asynchronous

# Page 36^page=36
13.36 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition I/O Software Layers u IO software are organized into layers u Each layer has a(from soft. Eng.): Ø Well-defined function to perform Ø Well-defined interface to the adjacent layer u Exact division differs by systems Hardware Interrupt handlers Device drivers Device independent operating system software User-level I/O software I/O Software Layer

# Page 37^page=37
13.37 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 用户进程 独立于设备的软件 设备驱动程序 中断处理程序 硬件 I / O 请 求 I / O完 成后的 回答 I / O软件的层次 唤醒设备驱动程序 进程，进行中断处 理 物理I/O操作 I/O系统调用，数据格 式化,Spooling 设备命名 、设备保 护、成块 处理、缓 冲技术、 设备分配 与释放 设置设备 寄存器、 检查设备 的执行状 态

# Page 38^page=38
13.38 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Interrupt Handlers

# Page 39^page=39
13.39 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Drivers u The software that talks to the controller u Device specific, Tailored(定制)to individual device characteristics u Written by device manufacturers u Part of the OS Kernel u Know about the details of the devices Ø Disk driver knows about sectors, tracks, cylinders, heads, arm motions, motor drives Ø Mouse driver knows about button pressed

# Page 40^page=40
13.40 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Drivers u Device drivers’ tasks: Ø Accept abstract read/write requests 4from device-independent software above it Ø See that(确保)read/write requests carried out Ø Initialize device, turn on/off device Ø Power management for device u Most OS define a standard interfaces for Ø Block devices Ø Character devices

# Page 41^page=41
13.41 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Drivers u On UNIX, Ø OS is a single binary with device drivers compiled into it Ø Add new drivers or modify existing ones means recompile OS Ø But Solaris 10 has made device driver loadable afterwards u In Windows, drivers are dynamically loaded

# Page 42^page=42
13.42 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Driver Operation u Block itself and wait for interrupt u Upon waking up, check for I/O errors u Pass data to the layer above it u Return status to its caller

# Page 43^page=43
13.43 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device-Independent I/O Software uSome I/O software is device specific，but some are not! uBasic function is to: Ø Perform IO ops that are common to all devices Ø Provide uniform interface to user-level software

# Page 44^page=44
13.44 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Functions of the device-independent I/O software Uniform interfacing for device drivers Buffering Error reporting Allocating and releasing dedicate devices Providing a device-independent block size Device-Independent I/O Software Exact boundary between drivers and device independent software is device dependent

# Page 45^page=45
13.45 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Uniform Interfacing u Make all devices look the same or similar Ø By standardizing driver interface u Specify the functions a driver provides and kernel function a driver can call Without a standard driver interface With a standard driver interface

# Page 46^page=46
13.46 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Independence uProblem: Ø lots of different brands of disks, disk interfaces, Ø And disk controllers Ø each with their own custom control interface uSame is true of any I/O device Ø network card, video card, keyboard, mouse, etc.

# Page 47^page=47
13.47 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device Independence u Add a “device driver” layer: to hide this diversity（差异）/complexity u An abstraction makes things “better” : for things that use the abstraction u How to use devices for user? Ø Logical Unit table: Maps logical devices to physical devices LUT(Logical Unit Table) 逻辑设备名 物理设备名 驱动程序入口地址 /dev/tty 3 1024 /dev/print 5 2046 … … … 逻辑设备名 系统设备表指针 /dev/tty 3 /dev/print 5 … … Millions of hardware devices Rest of operating systems Device drivers Physical interface Virtual interface

# Page 48^page=48
13.48 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition I/O system Layers and their main functions User-Space I/O Software

# Page 49^page=49
13.49 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition User-Space I/O Software uIt is often convenient for some part of I/O software to run in the user space(such library) uMost OS do put part of IO in user space! uEx: Count = write(fd, buffer, nbytes) uLibrary routine write will be linked with the program and contained in the binary program present in memory at run time

# Page 50^page=50
13.50 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition User-Space I/O Software u Other I/O functions in user space include: Ø Spooling: Spooling daemon runs as a user process Spooling(Simultaneous Peripheral Operation On-Line)

# Page 51^page=51
13.51 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Buffering uBuffering--store data in memory while transferring between devices pTo cope with device speed mismatch pTo cope with device transfer size mismatch pTo maintain “copy semantics” （保证写入盘的数据就是Write()系统调用发生时的版本）

# Page 52^page=52
13.52 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Sun Enterprise 6000 Device-Transfer Rates 千兆

# Page 53^page=53
13.53 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Buffering Unbuffered Buffer in user space Buffer in kernel Double buffer followed by in the kernel copying to user space

# Page 54^page=54
13.54 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Drawback of Buffering Networking may involve many copies Ü Slows down performance

# Page 55^page=55
13.55 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition I/O Scheduling uI/O Scheduling Ø Some I/O request ordering via per-device queue Ø Some OSs try fairness uSuch as Disk Scheduling

# Page 56^page=56
13.56 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition u Data structures about device Ø 设备控制表DCT Ø 控制器控制表COCT Ø 通道控制表CHCT Ø 系统设备表 SDT Device Allocation u Device reservation - provides exclusive access to a device Ø Another way to deal with concurrent device access. Ø System calls for allocation and deallocation

13.58 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 设备分配步骤 系 统 查 L UT 表 查 SDT 表 查 DCT 表 该 设 备 忙 否 ? 分 配 设 备 查 COCT 表 该 控 制 器 忙 否 ? 分 配 控 制 器 查 CHCT 表 该 通 道 忙 否 ? 分 配 通 道 该 用 户 进 程 插 入 设 备 等 待 队 列 该 用 户 进 程 插 入 通 道 等 待 队 列 Y N N N Y Y 安 全 否 ? 最 后 一 个 DCT ? N YN Y 该 用 户 进 程 插 入 控 制 器 等 待 队 列 最 后 一 个 COCT ? N Y 最 后 一 个 CHCT ? N Y

13.59 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Device-status Table

# Page 60^page=60
13.60 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Kernel Data Structures u Kernel keeps state info for I/O components, including open file tables, network connections, character device state u Many, many complex data structures to track buffers, memory allocation, “dirty” blocks u Some use object-oriented methods and message passing to implement I/O Ø Windows NT, an I/O request is converted into a message through the kernel to the manager and then to the device driver.

# Page 61^page=61
13.61 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition UNIX I/O Kernel Structure

# Page 62^page=62
13.62 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Use of a System Call to Perform I/O I/O Protection u User process may accidentally or purposefully attempt to disrupt normal operation via illegal I/O instructions (无意或有意地通过非法指令破坏正常操作) p All I/O instructions defined to be privileged p I/O must be performed via system calls 4 Memory-mapped and I/O port memory locations must be protected too

# Page 63^page=63
13.63 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Error Reporting u Errors far more common in I/O Ø Due to interfacing with outside world u Errors inside the computer case are rare u Error reporting depends on types of error u Programming errors Ø Process ask for something impossible Ø Read from an output device u Actual I/O errors Ø Accessing a damaged part of disk

# Page 64^page=64
13.64 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Error Reporting uFor programming errors, just report error back to the caller uFor actual I/O errors, Ø May take corrective measures（纠正措施） Ø May ask the caller what to do Ø May simply fail and return an error code

# Page 65^page=65
13.65 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition I/O Requests to Hardware Operations uConsider reading a file from disk for a process: p Determine device holding file p Translate name to device representation p Physically read data from disk into buffer p Make data available to requesting process p Return control to process

# Page 66^page=66
13.66 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Life Cycle of An I/O Request

# Page 67^page=67
13.67 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A typical lifecycle of a blocking read request u A process issues a blocking read system call to a file descriptor of a file that has been opened previously.进程发出阻塞读的系统调用 u The system-call code in the kernel checks the parameters for correctness.in the case of input,if the data are already available in the buffer cache,the data are returned to the process and the I/O request is completed.系统检查参数的合法 性，如果要读的数据已经在缓冲区中，则将数据返回给用户进程。 u If the data are not in the buffer cache, a physical I/O needs to be performed,so the process is removed from the run queue and is placed on the wait queue for the device, and the I/O request is scheduled .eventually,the I/O subsystem sends the request to the device driver.depending on the operating system, the request is sent via a subroutine call or via an in-kernel message.否则，需启 动设备进行物理I/O。进程进入设备的等待队列，该I/O请求等待调度。最终I/O 子系统把这个I/O请求发给了设备驱动程序

# Page 68^page=68
13.68 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A typical lifecycle of a blocking read request u The device driver allocates kernel buffer space to receive the data, and schedules the I/O, eventually,the driver sends commands to the device controller by writing into the device control registers.设备驱动程序分配用于 接收数据的内核缓冲，并调度I/O。最终，驱动程序把该读命令写入控制器的 设备控制寄存器中 u The device controller operates the device hardware to perform the data transfer.设备控制器操纵设备完成数据传输 u The driver may poll for status and data. Or it may have set up a DMA transfer into kernel memory. We assume that the transfer is managed by a DMA controller,which generates an interrupt when the transfer completes.驱动程 序轮询设备状态或启动DMA传输。这里假定采用的是DMA方式

# Page 69^page=69
13.69 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A typical lifecycle of a blocking read request u The appropriate interrupt handler receives the interrupt via the interrupt-vector table,store any necessary data,signals the device driver,and returns from the interrupt.中断处理程序接收到中断信号，保 存相应的数据，并唤醒驱动程序 u The device driver receives the signal,determines which I/O request completed,determines the request’s status,and signals the kernel I/O subsystem that the request has been completed.驱动程序确定I/O请求完成了，并 确定请求的状态，然后告诉I/O子系统I/O请求已完成

# Page 70^page=70
13.70 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition A typical lifecycle of a blocking read request u The kernel transfers data or return codes to the address space of the requesting process,and moves the process from the wait queue back to the ready queue.内核把数据传 给用户进程，把该进程从等待队列中移入就绪队列 u The process is unblocked when it is moved to the ready queue.when the scheduler assigns the process to the CPU,the process resumes execution at the completion of the system call. 接下来，如果调度程序分派CPU给该进程，则该进程可以恢 复执行
